text:
Once upon a moonlit night, there was a quaint little town named Whimsy. Everyone spoke with charming whimsy, like they were plucking feathers from a hummingbird. In Whimsy lived a little fox named Felix.

Felix loved collecting antique spoons. He’d sneak into neighbors’ kitchens during lazy afternoons to fill his pouch with treasures like old ship captains’ forks and rusty spoonfuls of honey.

One day, while savoring a particularly shiny spoon, Felix overheard a gossiping bird at the pet shop.

“Have you seen that new parrot?”

“No, I’d die of laughter if I saw that!”

“How could you?”

“You keep stealing its sunglasses.”

The bird chuckled until its laughter pierced the quiet night. Felix flew in, thinking of jokes.

“Why don’t parrots read books?”

“You’re too busy reading sunflower seeds!”

Felix laughed heartily. Then a real joke slipped out:

“What do you call an exotic flamingo
